original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'claude', 'fast', 'claude') returns { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' } when models.claude.fast is 'claude-haiku-4-5'
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'claude', 'fast', 'claude') returns { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' } when models.claude.fast is 'claude-haiku-4-5'

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', 'claude') returns codex's fast model when models.codex.fast exists
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', 'claude') returns codex's fast model when models.codex.fast exists

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'unknown-agent', 'fast', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.fast (defaultAgent fallback)
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'unknown-agent', 'fast', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.fast (defaultAgent fallback)

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'powerful', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.powerful when models.codex has no powerful tier
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'powerful', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.powerful when models.codex has no powerful tier

original: When legacy flat config { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } is loaded, NaxConfigSchema.parse() auto-migrates to { claude: { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } } using defaultAgent
refined: When legacy flat config { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } is loaded, NaxConfigSchema.parse() auto-migrates to { claude: { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } } using defaultAgent

original: When new per-agent config { claude: { fast: 'haiku' }, codex: { fast: 'gpt-5' } } is loaded, NaxConfigSchema.parse() preserves it unchanged
refined: When new per-agent config { claude: { fast: 'haiku' }, codex: { fast: 'gpt-5' } } is loaded, NaxConfigSchema.parse() preserves it unchanged

original: TierConfig accepts optional agent field — { tier: 'fast', attempts: 3, agent: 'codex' } passes Zod validation
refined: TierConfig accepts optional agent field — { tier: 'fast', attempts: 3, agent: 'codex' } passes Zod validation

original: TierConfig without agent field passes Zod validation (backward compat)
refined: TierConfig without agent field passes Zod validation (backward compat)

original: DEFAULT_CONFIG.models is { claude: { fast: 'haiku', balanced: 'sonnet', powerful: 'opus' } } and DEFAULT_CONFIG.autoMode.fallbackOrder is ['claude']
refined: DEFAULT_CONFIG.models is { claude: { fast: 'haiku', balanced: 'sonnet', powerful: 'opus' } } and DEFAULT_CONFIG.autoMode.fallbackOrder is ['claude']

original: validate() returns error when fallbackOrder contains agent 'codex' but models has no 'codex' key
refined: validate() returns error when fallbackOrder contains agent 'codex' but models has no 'codex' key

original: validate() returns error when tierOrder has { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } but models has no 'codex' key
refined: validate() returns error when tierOrder has { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } but models has no 'codex' key

original: ModelsConfig is typed as Record<string, Record<ModelTier, ModelEntry>>
refined: ModelsConfig is typed as Record<string, Record<ModelTier, ModelEntry>>

original: TierConfig accepts optional agent field — { tier: 'fast', attempts: 3, agent: 'codex' } is valid
refined: TierConfig accepts optional agent field — { tier: 'fast', attempts: 3, agent: 'codex' } is valid

original: TierConfig without agent field remains valid (backward compat)
refined: TierConfig without agent field remains valid (backward compat)

original: StoryRouting has optional agent field typed as string
refined: StoryRouting has optional agent field typed as string

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'claude', 'fast', 'claude') returns { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' } when models.claude.fast is 'claude-haiku-4-5'
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'claude', 'fast', 'claude') returns { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'claude-haiku-4-5' } when models.claude.fast is 'claude-haiku-4-5'

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', 'claude') returns codex's fast model when models.codex.fast exists
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', 'claude') returns codex's fast model when models.codex.fast exists

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'unknown-agent', 'fast', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.fast
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'unknown-agent', 'fast', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.fast

original: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'powerful', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.powerful when models.codex has no powerful tier
refined: resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'powerful', 'claude') falls back to models.claude.powerful when models.codex has no powerful tier

original: Throws NaxError MODEL_NOT_FOUND when neither agent nor defaultAgent has the requested tier
refined: Throws NaxError MODEL_NOT_FOUND when neither agent nor defaultAgent has the requested tier

original: When legacy flat config { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } is parsed, NaxConfigSchema.parse() auto-migrates to { claude: { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } } using defaultAgent
refined: When legacy flat config { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } is parsed, NaxConfigSchema.parse() auto-migrates to { claude: { fast: { provider: 'anthropic', model: 'haiku' } } } using defaultAgent

original: When new per-agent config { claude: { fast: 'haiku' }, codex: { fast: 'gpt-5' } } is parsed, NaxConfigSchema.parse() preserves it unchanged
refined: When new per-agent config { claude: { fast: 'haiku' }, codex: { fast: 'gpt-5' } } is parsed, NaxConfigSchema.parse() preserves it unchanged

original: DEFAULT_CONFIG.models is { claude: { fast: 'haiku', balanced: 'sonnet', powerful: 'opus' } }
refined: DEFAULT_CONFIG.models is { claude: { fast: 'haiku', balanced: 'sonnet', powerful: 'opus' } }

original: DEFAULT_CONFIG.autoMode.fallbackOrder is ['claude']
refined: DEFAULT_CONFIG.autoMode.fallbackOrder is ['claude']

original: config-descriptions.ts descriptions mention per-agent map shape
refined: config-descriptions.ts descriptions mention per-agent map shape

original: validate() returns error when fallbackOrder contains 'codex' but models has no 'codex' key
refined: validate() returns error when fallbackOrder contains 'codex' but models has no 'codex' key

original: validate() returns error when tierOrder has { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } but models has no 'codex' key
refined: validate() returns error when tierOrder has { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } but models has no 'codex' key

original: validate() passes when all fallbackOrder agents and tierOrder agents exist in models
refined: validate() passes when all fallbackOrder agents and tierOrder agents exist in models

original: When config.models has only claude agent and defaultAgent is 'claude', all callsites behave identically to the old flat config.models[tier] pattern
refined: When config.models has only claude agent and defaultAgent is 'claude', all callsites behave identically to the old flat config.models[tier] pattern

original: When ctx.routing.agent is 'codex' and config.models.codex.fast exists, execution.ts resolves to codex's fast model instead of claude's
refined: When ctx.routing.agent is 'codex' and config.models.codex.fast exists, execution.ts resolves to codex's fast model instead of claude's

original: When ctx.routing.agent is unset, all callsites resolve using config.autoMode.defaultAgent
refined: When ctx.routing.agent is unset, all callsites resolve using config.autoMode.defaultAgent

original: metrics/tracker.ts records agentUsed (the resolved agent name) alongside modelTier and modelUsed in story metrics
refined: metrics/tracker.ts records agentUsed (the resolved agent name) alongside modelTier and modelUsed in story metrics

original: config/validate.ts validates that every agent in fallbackOrder and every tierOrder[].agent exists as a key in models
refined: config/validate.ts validates that every agent in fallbackOrder and every tierOrder[].agent exists as a key in models

original: The old resolveModel(config.models[tier]) pattern no longer appears in any source file under src/
refined: The old resolveModel(config.models[tier]) pattern no longer appears in any source file under src/

original: When adapter.complete() receives a 429 response from acpx, it automatically retries with the next agent in fallbackOrder at the same tier — caller receives the successful response without knowing a fallback occurred
refined: When adapter.complete() receives a 429 response from acpx, it automatically retries with the next agent in fallbackOrder at the same tier — caller receives the successful response without knowing a fallback occurred

original: When adapter.run() receives stderr containing 'rate limit' or '429', it automatically retries with the next agent in fallbackOrder
refined: When adapter.run() receives stderr containing 'rate limit' or '429', it automatically retries with the next agent in fallbackOrder

original: When adapter.complete() receives a 401/403 auth error, it retries with next agent in fallbackOrder AND marks the failing agent unavailable for subsequent calls (this._unavailableAgents)
refined: When adapter.complete() receives a 401/403 auth error, it retries with next agent in fallbackOrder AND marks the failing agent unavailable for subsequent calls (this._unavailableAgents)

original: Fallback does NOT decrement the story's attempt count
refined: Fallback does NOT decrement the story's attempt count

original: When all agents in fallbackOrder return rate-limit errors, adapter waits min(retryAfterSeconds) seconds (or 30s default) then retries from fallbackOrder[0]
refined: When all agents in fallbackOrder return rate-limit errors, adapter waits min(retryAfterSeconds) seconds (or 30s default) then retries from fallbackOrder[0]

original: When fallbackOrder has only one agent and it's rate-limited, adapter waits retryAfterSeconds (or 30s default) then retries the same agent
refined: When fallbackOrder has only one agent and it's rate-limited, adapter waits retryAfterSeconds (or 30s default) then retries the same agent

original: When all agents are permanently unavailable (auth errors on every agent), adapter throws AllAgentsUnavailableError
refined: When all agents are permanently unavailable (auth errors on every agent), adapter throws AllAgentsUnavailableError

original: Fallback events are logged at info level with stage 'agent-fallback' including: original agent, fallback agent, error type, retry count
refined: Fallback events are logged at info level with stage 'agent-fallback' including: original agent, fallback agent, error type, retry count

original: CLI adapter (src/agents/cli/) is unchanged — no fallback logic added
refined: CLI adapter (src/agents/cli/) is unchanged — no fallback logic added

original: escalateTier('fast', tierOrder) returns { tier: 'balanced', agent: 'claude' } when tierOrder is [{ tier: 'fast', agent: 'claude', attempts: 3 }, { tier: 'balanced', agent: 'claude', attempts: 2 }]
refined: escalateTier('fast', tierOrder) returns { tier: 'balanced', agent: 'claude' } when tierOrder is [{ tier: 'fast', agent: 'claude', attempts: 3 }, { tier: 'balanced', agent: 'claude', attempts: 2 }]

original: escalateTier('balanced', tierOrder) returns { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } when next entry is { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex', attempts: 2 }
refined: escalateTier('balanced', tierOrder) returns { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex' } when next entry is { tier: 'fast', agent: 'codex', attempts: 2 }

original: escalateTier('balanced', tierOrder) returns null when at last tier entry
refined: escalateTier('balanced', tierOrder) returns null when at last tier entry

original: When tierOrder entry has no agent field, escalateTier() returns { tier: nextTier, agent: undefined } and caller uses defaultAgent
refined: When tierOrder entry has no agent field, escalateTier() returns { tier: nextTier, agent: undefined } and caller uses defaultAgent

original: handlePreIterationEscalation() sets ctx.routing.agent to the escalated tier's agent when present
refined: handlePreIterationEscalation() sets ctx.routing.agent to the escalated tier's agent when present

original: After escalation from claude/balanced to codex/fast, execution stage calls resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', defaultAgent)
refined: After escalation from claude/balanced to codex/fast, execution stage calls resolveModelForAgent(models, 'codex', 'fast', defaultAgent)
